title: Milestone 3: Data App
subtitle: Handling data like a Pro.
goal: Build an app that uses OOP and Collections.

blocks.heading: Persistent Data
blocks.html: Your next project is a Contact Book. You've learned about Classes, Encapsulation, Exceptions, and ArrayLists. It's time to put them together.

blocks.heading: Requirements
blocks.html: Create a Contact class (with name, phone, email). Create a Main.java that uses an ArrayList<Contact> to let the user add, view, and search contacts.

blocks.heading: Hints & Tips
blocks.html:
• Constructors: Your Contact class should have a constructor that sets the fields.
  • toString(): Override toString() in Contact so printing it looks nice!
  • Search: Use a loop to check if a contact's name .contains() the search term.

blocks.heading: Share your code
blocks.html: Commit and push to GitHub. You are building a real Java portfolio now!

challenge.title: Complete your Contact Book
challenge.instructions: Build the app on your computer and push it to GitHub.
challenge.solution: public class Main { public static void main(String[] args) {} }
challenge.passMsg: 🏆 You are mastering OOP and Collections!
